notes: Complete re-architecture of BusinessObjects; integrated Reporter, Explorer, Document Agent Server, Designer, Supervisor modules; foundation for subsequent releases; became SAP BusinessObjects after 2008
Client-side multidimensional analysis engine; builds compact microcubes on-the-fly from SQL queries; eliminates pre-built cube requirement; concept evolved into modern in-memory analytics
Business representation of database hiding SQL complexity; drag-and-drop metaphor for IS and business users; concept lives on in SAP HANA and modern BI semantic layers
Three-tier web application server to bring BusinessObjects functionality to WWW via Java applets; became WebIntelligence upon release; concept evolved into web-based BI standard
Desktop decision-tree data mining tool licensed from ISoft; $995 standalone or $495 with BusinessObjects 4.0; discontinued; data mining integrated into broader BI platforms
Third-tier component for scheduled report updating and distribution; $4,995; concept evolved into modern BI server and report scheduling
Multidimensional data analysis; BusinessObjects 4.0 brought OLAP to mainstream users without proprietary OLAP databases
Pattern discovery in large datasets; Aberdeen predicted democratization of data mining from specialists to business analysts; prediction proved accurate by 2010s
OLAP analysis on top of relational databases; BusinessObjects schemaless approach allowed this without star/snowflake schema requirements
Project Darwin planned to use Java applets for web delivery of BI; Java applets deprecated by browsers by 2017; concept succeeded through HTML5 and modern web standards
Broad category for analytics and reporting tools; Business Objects was a founding vendor of the category
